results.agent_response: I can help you with your data integration setup. Could you provide more details about what you're trying to accomplish?

missing_signals: Before re-running, ask what error the sync failed with — re-running without fixing the root cause will fail again
If the failure reason is known and fixed: trigger a manual sync from the connector settings using the 'Run now' or 'Sync now' option
Note that Redshift syncs can fail silently on IAM permission errors — always check the sync logs, not just the overall status indicator
If the error involves 'connection refused' or 'timeout': verify the Redshift cluster is running and not paused (Redshift Serverless autopause can cause this)

results.agent_response: I can help you with your data integration setup. Could you provide more details about what you're trying to accomplish?

missing_signals: Ask whether the tables are newly missing (appeared before but disappeared) or never appeared at all — these have different root causes
For tables that never appeared: check whether the PostgreSQL schema configured in the connector includes those tables, and whether the connector user has SELECT privilege on them
For tables that disappeared: check whether the connector's schema scope configuration changed, or whether the tables were dropped or renamed in PostgreSQL
Ask if the tables follow a naming pattern (e.g., temp_, _backup) that might match an exclusion filter in the connector configuration
